title: Write privacy and artwork copy
overview: Expand the info overlay copy from a single sentence to concise multi-paragraph text that explains EAVI as an art installation, its privacy model (no data stored), and how visitor context shapes the scene. Update existing tests to validate the new copy requirements.

steps.description: Write tests that assert the overlay copy meets acceptance criteria
details: Add a new describe block 'US-022: Privacy and artwork copy'
Test: overlay text contains language identifying EAVI as an art installation (match /art\s*installation/i or similar)
Test: overlay text states no data is stored (match /no.*(data|information).*(stored|saved|retained)/i or similar)
Test: overlay text states visitor context influences the scene (match /visitor.*(context|presence).*(influence|shape)/i or similar)
Test: total copy length stays concise — e.g. under 500 characters total across all paragraphs
Test: overlay still contains the existing general description of EAVI as an ephemeral experience

steps.description: Replace single paragraph with structured copy in the overlay
details:
Replace the single `

` element in createInfoOverlay with multiple short paragraphs or a small set of elements
Paragraph 1: Identify EAVI as an ephemeral generative art installation
Paragraph 2: State the privacy model — no cookies, no storage, no tracking; nothing is saved
Paragraph 3: Explain that visitor context (time, device, movement) subtly shapes the scene
Keep total copy under ~500 characters; use plain, non-legal language per ticket notes
Preserve existing DOM structure: panel > closeBtn + content elements

steps.description: Run tests and build to confirm everything passes
details: Run `npx vitest run` — all tests pass including new US-022 tests
Run `npx vite build` — build succeeds with no errors
Manual check: no console errors, overlay reads well, concise and accurate

acceptance_checklist: Overlay states this is an art installation
Overlay states no data is stored
Overlay states visitor context influences the scene
Language is simple and avoids legal overstatement
notes: This is a copy-only change to an existing UI component. No new files, no structural changes. The overlay shell and toggle behavior from US-021 remain untouched.
